{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents discuss various sterilization methods, including dry heat. Dry heat sterilization involves exposing microorganisms to high temperatures, which denature proteins and destroy the microorganisms. This process is similar to cooking an egg, where the proteins unfold and become unusable. None of the documents mention lysis, oxidation, hydrolysis of DNA, or coagulation of proteins as the mechanism for dry heat sterilization.", "answer_choice": "D"}
